audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Deuteronomy / Devarim unsolved-murder, captive-woman, firstborn, rebellious-son, and corpse-dignity law, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for communal bloodguilt, heifer rite, captive woman and mourning period, hated-wife firstborn rights, rebellious-son execution, hanging, same-day burial, and land defilement.

verses.restored: If one slain is found on ground YHWH your Divine gives you to possess, fallen in field, and it is not known who struck him,
then your elders and judges shall go out and measure to cities around the slain one.
It shall be, the city nearest to slain one—elders of that city shall take a heifer from herd that has not been worked and has not drawn in yoke.
Elders of that city shall bring heifer down to a perennial/rough valley that is not worked or sown, and there they shall break neck of heifer in valley.
Levitical priests shall draw near, for them YHWH your Divine chose to serve him and bless in name of YHWH, and by their mouth every dispute and every lesion shall be decided.
All elders of that city nearest to slain one shall wash their hands over heifer whose neck was broken in valley.
They shall answer and say, Our hands did not shed this blood, and our eyes did not see.
Make atonement/covering for your people Israel whom you redeemed, YHWH, and do not place innocent blood in midst of your people Israel; and blood shall be covered for them.
So you shall purge innocent blood from your midst, when you do what is right in eyes of YHWH.
When you go out to battle against your enemies and YHWH your Divine gives them into your hand and you take captives,
and you see among captives a woman beautiful of form, and you desire her and would take her for yourself as wife,
then you shall bring her into midst of your house; she shall shave her head and tend/cut her nails.

She shall remove garment of her captivity from upon her and dwell in your house and weep for her father and mother a month of days; afterward you may go in to her and become her husband, and she shall become your wife.

It shall be, if you do not delight in her, you shall send her away according to her soul/desire; you shall surely not sell her for silver, and you shall not treat her as merchandise, because you humbled/violated her.
If a man has two wives, one loved and one hated, and they bear him sons, loved and hated, and firstborn son belongs to hated one,
then on day he causes his sons to inherit what is his, he may not make son of loved one firstborn over son of hated one, the firstborn.
But firstborn, son of hated one, he shall recognize, giving him double portion of all found with him, for he is first of his strength; right of firstborn is his.
If a man has a stubborn and rebellious son who does not listen to voice of his father or voice of his mother, and they discipline him and he does not listen to them,
then his father and mother shall seize him and bring him out to elders of his city and gate of his place.
They shall say to elders of his city, This our son is stubborn and rebellious; he does not listen to our voice; a glutton and drunkard.
Then all men of his city shall stone him with stones and he shall die; so you shall remove evil from your midst, and all Israel shall hear and fear/revere.
If there is in a man sin/misalignment deserving judgment of death, and he is put to death and you hang him upon tree,

his corpse shall not remain all night upon tree, but you shall surely bury him that day, for one hanged is a curse of Divine; you shall not defile your ground YHWH your Divine gives you as inheritance.

translation_notes: The unsolved-murder rite treats unidentified violence as a communal crisis rather than a private tragedy; leaders must publicly account for innocent blood.
Nachal eitan in verse 4 is lexically uncertain and may indicate a perennial stream valley or a rough, enduring ravine.

The captive-woman law restrains immediate seizure, permits mourning, and forbids resale, but it still operates inside war captivity without meaningful consent. It must not be romanticized as marriage protection.
The word rendered humbled/violated in verse 14 acknowledges harm and may carry sexual-coercion implications.
The firstborn right protects the child of a disfavored woman from paternal manipulation.
The rebellious-son execution is an extreme child-killing law. Later Jewish interpretation famously narrowed it toward practical impossibility, but the source remains morally dangerous.
Same-day burial limits postmortem humiliation and protects dignity even after execution.
safety_note: Never use Deuteronomy 21 to justify captive marriage, rape, trafficking, forced marriage, child execution, parental violence, corporal punishment, or public desecration of bodies. Center accountability for unsolved death, protection against inheritance favoritism, freedom from resale, and the dignity of prompt burial.
